acceptance_tests: openpyxl can parse crash_cluster_3.xlsx and reproduce per-record crash_after and totals
expert_memo.md numeric values match the workbook exactly and cell addresses present for key claims
CSV export matches workbook values and is attached
agent_trace.json validates against canonical schema with no normalization errors
extra_model_fields.post_revision_notes_for_verifier: Include the verification log and the CSV export. If any feasibility flags changed as a result of corrected arithmetic, document the change and the impact on recommended plan selection.

planned_changes: Open crash_cluster_3.xlsx locally and recompute all remediation-cost combination sums; update any misreported totals (O1+O3, O1+O2+O3, etc.) in the workbook and the memo.

Ensure derived columns (per-record crash_after, class_at_threshold for each sweep value, unstable_flag, Total remediation cost) are implemented as live spreadsheet formulas rather than static values. Label those cells clearly and add comments with formula text where helpful.
Add explicit cell references in the memo for each key reported value (e.g., 'Sheet1!G2 = crash_after for R2 under O3').
Export the main sheets used for verification to CSV (crash_cluster_3.parsed_tables.csv) and attach them as additional artifacts to the attempt.

If openpyxl environment limitations persist, include high-resolution screenshots of the formula bar for the critical cells (packaged as crash_cluster_3_formula_screenshots.zip) and a short text file enumerating the formulas.

Add a short, explicit recomputation example row in expert_memo.md demonstrating the threshold comparison for R2 under O3 (include formulas and expected boolean outcome) so the unstable flip is trivial to reproduce programmatically.
Correct the recommendation table so post-action review sets align with recomputed results and clearly call out which records are stable vs unstable after each plan.

Validate that the updated agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json fully conform to the AgentTrace canonical schema; reserialize the trace if necessary to remove normalization errors and include explicit success/error fields for each step.

Run an automated local verification: openpyxl parse of the updated .xlsx, compare workbook computed totals against memo numbers, and include a short verification log in the attempt artifacts documenting the parse success and any changes made.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Fix numeric arithmetic errors and verifiability gaps so the deliverables are machine-verifiable and internally consistent: correct memo totals, expose live spreadsheet formulas and cell addresses, provide programmatic exports (CSV) or screenshots for verification, correct recommendation table inconsistencies, and produce a normalized agent_trace.json.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables are present and mostly complete, but there are important verification and correctness issues to fix before accepting the attempt: (1) arithmetic inconsistencies in the memo's combination totals (ri_1) reduce trust in numeric correctness; (2) the .xlsx could not be programmatically parsed (openpyxl missing), so claims that rely on live spreadsheet formulas are not verifiable; (3) the recommendation table has minor consistency errors in the post-action review sets; (4) the agent_trace/actual_outputs evidence contains normalization/trace-writing irregularities that made trace normalization fail. These issues must be resolved to ensure the deliverable is machine-verifiable and unambiguous.
